Metropolitan Washington rapid transit system : environmental impact statement.

The proposal was to extend the Metro 'A' Route from Rockville to Shady Grove in Montgomery County, Maryland. This involved moving the terminal station and service yard to Shady Grove, adding 2.66 miles of track. The project aimed to improve transit accessibility, potentially increasing ridership and reducing vehicle emissions. However, it would transform a rural area into an industrial landscape, impacting local ecosystems and increasing traffic congestion. The community faced changes in land use and potential environmental degradation, particularly affecting Crabbs Branch's water quality.
Shady Grove to Rockville, Montgomery County, Maryland

The proposal to extend the Metro 'A' Route from Rockville to Shady Grove was driven by the need to enhance public transit accessibility in Montgomery County, Maryland. While the project aimed to improve ridership and reduce vehicle emissions, it faced significant public pushback due to concerns about environmental impacts and changes to local land use. Ultimately, the project was completed, but it sparked ongoing discussions about balancing infrastructure development with environmental preservation.
United States. Urban Mass Transportation Administration

"The permanent adverse environmental impact will be the transformation from a rural to an industrial landscape which will involve clearing, regrading, building and paving on a 70-acre tract in the headwaters of Crabbs Branch." — Summary of environmental impact and adverse effects
